---
title: "Variables"
---

Variables (`vars`) let you centralize reusable values and keep task commands readable. They are especially useful when the same values are needed across many tasks, profiles, or templates.

## Variable Types

`qp` supports two styles:

1. Static value
2. Shell-resolved value (`sh`)

```yaml
vars:
  region: eu-west-1
  service: payments-api
  git_sha:
    sh: git rev-parse --short HEAD
```

Use static vars for stable defaults and `sh` vars for dynamic repo context.

## Using Variables In Tasks

Variables are referenced as `{{vars.<name>}}`.

```yaml
tasks:
  build:
    desc: Build service image
    cmd: docker build -t ghcr.io/acme/{{vars.service}}:{{vars.git_sha}} .

  deploy:
    desc: Deploy image to a region
    cmd: ./scripts/deploy.sh --region {{vars.region}} --image ghcr.io/acme/{{vars.service}}:{{vars.git_sha}}
```

```bash
qp build
qp deploy
```

## Overrides From Environment: `QP_VAR_*`

Any variable can be overridden at runtime with environment variables prefixed by `QP_VAR_`.

```bash
QP_VAR_REGION=us-east-1 qp deploy
QP_VAR_SERVICE=payments-worker qp build
```

`QP_VAR_REGION` overrides `vars.region`, `QP_VAR_SERVICE` overrides `vars.service`, and so on.

## Overrides From CLI: `--var name=value`

CLI overrides are explicit and script-friendly.

```bash
qp deploy --var region=us-east-1
qp build --var service=payments-worker --var git_sha=v2.4.1
```

## Precedence

Variable resolution order is:

1. `--var name=value` (highest)
2. `QP_VAR_NAME` environment override
3. `vars` in `qp.yaml` (including `sh`)

This means CLI flags always win for one-off runs.

## Worked Precedence Example

```yaml
vars:
  region: eu-west-1

tasks:
  show-region:
    desc: Print resolved region
    cmd: echo {{vars.region}}
```

```bash
qp show-region
# eu-west-1

QP_VAR_REGION=us-east-1 qp show-region
# us-east-1

QP_VAR_REGION=us-east-1 qp show-region --var region=ap-southeast-2
# ap-southeast-2
```

## Variables In Expressions

Vars can be used in CEL expressions through `vars`.

```yaml
tasks:
  deploy:
    desc: Deploy only in approved region
    cmd: ./scripts/deploy.sh
    when: vars.region == "eu-west-1" || vars.region == "us-east-1"
```

This pattern helps encode guardrails in config instead of shell logic.

## Variables In Task `env`

You can map variable values into environment variables for tools that expect env-based configuration.

```yaml
tasks:
  smoke:
    desc: Smoke test against selected region
    cmd: ./scripts/smoke.sh
    env:
      AWS_REGION: "{{vars.region}}"
      SERVICE_NAME: "{{vars.service}}"
```

## Practical Patterns

1. Put repo-wide defaults in `vars`.
2. Use profiles for environment bundles (staging/prod).
3. Keep CI explicit by passing `--var` in pipeline scripts.
4. Use `QP_VAR_*` for local experimentation without editing `qp.yaml`.

## Next Step

To avoid repeating command fragments and generate repeated task families, continue to [Templates](templates.qmd).
